summary: R-1 is not fully resolved: the plan requires persistent placeholder materialization but its schema cannot represent both the template and active value.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Open question: placeholder materialization has no schema representation
findings.body: The plan says the materialized value persists as active state and Compile consumes it, but schema v1 defines only one Modification value field and no materialized-value state. A competent implementation must guess whether to overwrite the template (preventing fresh generation on the next activation), store undeclared parallel state, or recompute it after restart—reintroducing R-1. Import/export and deactivation behavior are consequently also undefined.
findings.recommendation: Define separate persisted template and materialized-value fields, including creation on inactive→active, clearing on active→inactive, behavior when the template changes while active, and import/export handling. Add lifecycle tests covering restart, deactivate/reactivate, and active-profile editing.

next_steps: Amend the schema and lifecycle/testing decisions for placeholder state, then re-run the R-1 gate.
